_README: This file is HAND-EDITED ONLY. No script writes to it.
Each ratio enforces a structural property of PowDB that should not silently degrade.
Raising a ceiling means the thesis gave ground — justify it in the commit message.

powql_point_over_btree_lookup.meaning: Full PowQL parser+planner+executor overhead over the raw B-tree probe. This was originally a 2.5x ceiling (2.055x observed) when btree_lookup was 1832ns. Mission D + LTO dropped btree_lookup to 262ns (-85.68%) while powql_point only dropped to 1311ns (-65% from an implied pre-Mission-D ~3764ns). The DENOMINATOR shrank faster than the numerator — the absolute parser/planner overhead (~1049ns) is structurally unchanged; the ratio rose mechanically. Ceiling raised 2.5 -> 6.0 to reflect the new hardware-level numbers. If parser/planner overhead grows beyond ~1.6μs this ratio will cross 6x and re-trip. Tightening further requires either a plan cache hit that short-circuits the parser (future Mission) or re-targeting the thesis at absolute ns instead of a ratio.

scan_filter_count_over_btree_lookup.meaning: Sanity ceiling: a count-filter aggregate over 100K rows vs a single B-tree probe. Previous ceiling was 8000 (pre-CI-capture). Post-Mission-D10 on ubuntu-24.04: 545729ns / 262ns = 2079.734x. Tightened 8000 -> 2500 to reflect reality with ~20% headroom. A regression here almost certainly means the compiled predicate or raw-bytes walker got un-wired.

update_by_pk_over_powql_point.meaning: An indexed update should be within a small multiple of an indexed read. Previous ceiling was 15000 (pre-FASTPATH, when update_by_pk was an O(N) full-rescan). Mission C Phase 14 (update_pk prepared fast path) landed the Update(IndexScan) fold and collapsed the ratio from ~9130x to 1.218x (1596ns / 1311ns). Tightened 15000 -> 2.0 to lock in the structural win — any future regression that loses the Update(IndexScan) fold will blow past this instantly.

insert_single_over_btree_lookup.meaning: Insert hot path should stay within a small multiple of a B-tree probe. Previous ceilings: 10.0 (M1, observed 5.51x), 12.0 (post-Mission-D ubuntu-24.04, observed 10.871x). On the mission-parallel-integration branch (PR #8 GHA run 24225043310): 3112ns / 247ns = 12.603x. Same dynamic as powql_point_over_btree_lookup — btree_lookup keeps shrinking (262 -> 247, -5.75%) while insert_single's absolute change stayed within its own 10% threshold (2853 -> 3112, +9.08%). The mechanical effect of an improving denominator pushed the ratio over even though both endpoints are within their own absolute gates. Relaxed 12.0 -> 14.0 to leave ~10% headroom above the current 12.603x and absorb one more round of btree_lookup improvement before this re-trips. The goal remains catching parse+plan+exec regressions in the insert hot path, not pinning absolute nanoseconds — and the absolute gate on insert_single is still doing that job.
